exclusion_reason: Paper-auditor FAIL (gap 62%). Paper's preferred Table 3 Col 3 spec uses county-specific linear time trends + multi-dimensional FE structure (county FE + conception-year FE + month-of-year FE + year×month interactions) which the template cannot replicate. Same outcome, same data, but template-imposed FE structure absorbs much less variation. Same sign (positive) but magnitude not comparable. Excluded 2026-04-19 from comparable subsample (N=53).
title: Does Managed Care Affect Birth Outcomes? Evidence from Texas's Medicaid Managed Care Mandate

construct_vars: df <- merge(df, mc_merge, by = 'tx_code', all.x = TRUE)

original_result.source: Table 3 (after on Black mortality, with county-year controls)

notes: Texas MMC rollout. County-month panel (year_mo as continuous, converted to integer months). 71 treated counties (7 cohorts: 1993-2006), 179 never-treated. Outcome = Black infant mortality (blan_mort, scaled ×100). Weight = blan_tot_b (Black births in cell). Paper uses year+month+year-month FEs; template uses time_int (integer months) as single time FE — simplified vs paper's multi-dimensional time FEs. 108 time periods. Controls: ln_pop, ln_inc, ln_tran, unemp.
